options.label: (RECOMMENDED) Clean-pause; re-fire GL2 from the implementer stage when the 5h window has headroom (now reset).
Proceed into the window and accept a mid-pipeline 429/park (wastes partial opus spend; competes with interactive usage, I28).

recommendation: Park GL2 at the implementer boundary and clean-pause; re-fire the implementer + panel + gate now that the window has reset. Corpus is vaulted so the re-fire resumes at the implementer with zero re-authoring. W16 HEADLINE: a single critical task's full all-opus/xhigh pipeline (6 workers) does NOT fit in one 5-hour window — pace it across windows / re-check the governor between every stage.
repro: Test-author (opus-4-8/xhigh) completed OK; its held-out corpus (4 files / 26 tests) is vaulted + manifested (11 entries: 7 GL1 + 4 GL2); all 4 spec-ambiguities discharged (corpus_covers=both); 5 lessons injected. But the test-author ALONE drove the 5-hour rate window 0.01 -> 0.77 (W16). The remaining CRITICAL pipeline (implementer + 4-lens validator panel = 5 more opus-4-8/xhigh workers, each ~0.75 of a 5h window) then EXHAUSTED the 5h usage limit at the implementer boundary — the limit hit was hard enough to take down the Bash safety classifier. Operator confirms the limit has since reset (five_hour back to ~16%).

resolved.decision: park-until-window-headroom — operator invoked /build-pause after the 5h usage limit was hit at the implementer boundary; limit now reset, re-fire resumes at the implementer stage
